evals.query: Write a Kora Kotlin component test for UserService using the application graph. The test should get a real UserService and assert that createUser returns a user.
expected_behavior: Annotates the test class with @KoraAppTest(Application::class)
Injects UserService with @TestComponent on a lateinit var
Adds testImplementation("ru.tinkoff.kora:test-junit5") and ksp("ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors")
Does not construct UserService manually with its constructor or use reflection-based field injection instead of @TestComponent

evals.query: Test OrderService which depends on UserRepository. Mock the repository with MockK and verify the interaction.
expected_behavior: Combines @MockK with @TestComponent on a lateinit var to register the mock in the graph
Stubs behavior with every { ... } returns ... and checks calls with verify { ... }
Does not add a = mockk() initializer next to the @MockK field
Does not attach MockKExtension via @ExtendWith alongside @KoraAppTest

evals.query: Test a suspend function createUser on UserService whose UserRepository.save is also a suspend function. Use MockK for the mock.
expected_behavior: Wraps the test body in runTest { } from kotlinx-coroutines-test
Uses coEvery to stub the suspend repository call and coVerify to verify it
Keeps @MockK + @TestComponent for the repository mock
Does not use plain every/verify for suspend functions or blockingGet/Thread.sleep workarounds

evals.query: My Kora Kotlin test needs to point the db config at a local PostgreSQL by substituting POSTGRES_JDBC_URL, POSTGRES_USER and POSTGRES_PASS. How do I override config in the test?
expected_behavior: Implements KoraAppTestConfigModifier on the test class and overrides config()
Returns KoraConfigModification.ofSystemProperty(...).withSystemProperty(...) to substitute the placeholders
Notes the modifier must be on the class body, not supplied via the constructor
Does not hardcode credentials into application.conf or bypass KoraConfigModification with a custom property source

evals.query: In a Kora Kotlin test I need to add a Supplier<Int> that is not declared in the @KoraApp, and also replace an existing Supplier<Int> deriving from the original. How?
expected_behavior: Implements KoraAppTestGraphModifier and overrides graph()
Uses KoraGraphModification.create().addComponent(TypeRef.of(Supplier::class.java, Int::class.java), ...)
Uses replaceComponent(TypeRef.of(...)) { graph -> graph.getFirst(...) } to wrap the existing component
Does not register components through a factory method in the @KoraApp instead of the test graph modifier

evals.query: Write a Kora Kotlin integration test for UserRepository against a real PostgreSQL via Testcontainers with Flyway migrations.
expected_behavior: Annotates with @Testcontainers and declares a @Container @JvmStatic PostgreSQLContainer in a companion object
Implements KoraAppTestConfigModifier and feeds POSTGRES.jdbcUrl/username/password via KoraConfigModification.ofString plus withSystemProperty
Injects the repository or service with @TestComponent and points @KoraAppTest at the application graph
Does not mock the database or replace the real JDBC connection with an in-memory stub

evals.query: I need a test-only repository with deleteAll() and findAll() to clean up between tests in my Kora Kotlin service. Set up the TestApplication pattern.
expected_behavior: Declares @KoraApp interface TestApplication : Application with a nested @Repository : JdbcRepository carrying @Query methods
Marks the test-only component reachable from a @Root so it is not pruned from the graph
Enables ksp { arg("kora.app.submodule.enabled", "true") }, adds kspTest("ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors") and tasks.test { exclude("**/$*") }
Points @KoraAppTest at TestApplication::class rather than the production Application

evals.query: Our team uses Mockito, not MockK, in a mixed Java/Kotlin codebase. How do I mock a Kora component in a Kotlin test with Mockito?
expected_behavior: Combines @Mock with @TestComponent on a lateinit var and adds org.mockito.kotlin:mockito-kotlin
Stubs with whenever(...).thenReturn(...) and verifies with verify(...)
Does not attach MockitoExtension via @ExtendWith together with @KoraAppTest
Does not initialize the @Mock field with = mock()
